question_text: A student has two identical containers. Container A is filled with lead (density 11,340 kg/m³) and container B is filled with aluminium (density 2,700 kg/m³). Both containers hold exactly 1 m³ of material. Calculate the difference in mass between the two containers.
explanation: Mass = density × volume. Lead: 11,340 × 1 = 11,340 kg. Aluminium: 2,700 × 1 = 2,700 kg. Difference: 11,340 - 2,700 = 8,640 kg. Lead is much denser so it has significantly more mass in the same volume.
